why-version-control-matters.label: Why a Project Needs History
why-version-control-matters.summary: See what Git protects before learning its commands: a clear record of what changed, why it changed, and which version is trusted.

sc-1.title: Purpose of Version Control
sc-1.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a version control system like Git?
sc-1.hint: Think about how version control helps manage changes.

help.concept: Version control systems help manage changes to projects over time.
help.hint_1: Consider how version control tracks changes.
help.hint_2: Version control allows you to revert to previous states.

options.a: To manage changes and track project history
options.c: To increase the speed of development
options.d: To replace the need for project management tools

sc-2.title: Git vs. GitHub
sc-2.prompt: How does Git differ from GitHub?
sc-2.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: Version control preserves a sequence of project snapshots so changes can be explained and recovered without relying on duplicate folder names.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove details that do not match the requested concept.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

options.a: Git is a local version control system, while GitHub is a hosted platform for collaboration
options.b: Git and GitHub are the same
options.c: GitHub is a version control system, while Git is a platform for collaboration
options.d: Git is used for managing code, while GitHub is used for managing documents

mc-1.title: Benefits of Using Git
mc-1.prompt: Which of the following are benefits of using Git? Select all that apply.
mc-1.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: Version control preserves a sequence of project snapshots so changes can be explained and recovered without relying on duplicate folder names.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove details that do not match the requested concept.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

options.a: Tracks changes to files over time
options.b: Allows multiple people to work on the same project

dr-1.title: Steps in a Git Workflow
dr-1.prompt: Arrange the steps in a typical Git workflow in the correct order.
dr-1.hint: Read each piece and arrange them in the order the statement should be understood.

help.concept: The pieces should form a valid statement in a logical order.
help.hint_1: Start with the piece that introduces the idea or action.
help.hint_2: Place dependent pieces after the part they describe or complete.

fbc-1.title: Git Command for Initialization
fbc-1.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the command used to initialize a new Git repository.
fbc-1.hint: This command sets up a new repository in the current directory.

help.concept: The command to initialize a new Git repository is 'the missing term'.

fbc-1.template: The command to initialize a new Git repository is '[blank1]'.

fbc-2.title: Git Status Command
fbc-2.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the command that checks the status of your Git repository.
fbc-2.hint: This command shows the current state of the working directory and staging area.

help.concept: The command to check the status of a Git repository is 'the missing term'.

fbc-2.template: The command to check the status of a Git repository is '[blank1]'.

intro-memory.title: When “Final” Stops Meaning Final
intro-memory.bodyMarkdown:
A volunteer team is preparing a neighborhood event guide. By Friday, the shared folder contains `guide-final.md`, `guide-final-fixed.md`, and `guide-final-really-final.md`. Nobody can tell which copy includes the corrected phone number, and replacing one file erases the path that led there.

Git solves that problem by keeping named snapshots of a project. A snapshot does not replace the folder you work in; it records a trustworthy point in the project’s history. Later, you can ask who recorded it, read its message, and compare it with another snapshot.

The important idea is not “save more copies.” It is “keep one project and give its meaningful states a history.”

worked-example.title: A Timeline You Can Inspect
worked-example.bodyMarkdown:
Imagine the guide begins with one page, then gains an accessibility note, then corrects an address. A useful history reads like a short timeline:

```text
Correct library address
Add accessibility note
Start neighborhood guide
```

Each line represents a deliberate checkpoint, not an automatic save. The newest snapshot appears first, while earlier snapshots remain available for inspection.

Git works locally. GitHub is a separate hosting and collaboration service that can store Git repositories online. This course stays local so you can learn the repository model without accounts, network access, or remote commands.
